B1_domain_codomain.consequence: Injectivity of Phi_w is not ruled out by pigeonhole (domain smaller than codomain). Collisions remain possible; free-1 toys show collisions can occur with avg < 1.
B1_domain_codomain.name: deployed_C_nm_strictly_less_than_p_w
proof: Compute log2 C(n,m) by the exact product formula sum_{i=0}^{m-1} log2(n-i)-log2(i+1).
Subtract w log2 p. The result is negative by more than 18000 bits, hence C(n,m) < p^w.

B1_iff_injective.name: Mm_le_1_iff_Phi_injective_on_m_subsets
proof: If Phi_w is injective, every fiber has size <= 1, so M_m^{max} <= 1.
If M_m^{max} <= 1, then no two distinct m-subsets share a prefix, so Phi_w is injective.
B1_iff_injective.statement: Let Phi_w map m-subsets of D to their length-w monic high-coefficient prefixes. Then M_m^{max} := max_b |Phi_w^{-1}(b)| is <= 1 if and only if Phi_w is injective on the set of all m-subsets of D.

B1_large_free_count.name: large_free_affine_parameter_count
B1_large_free_count.note: This is the correct large-free parameter count; it does not give uniqueness.
proof: Write Lambda = X^m + z_1 X^{m-1}+...+z_w X^{m-w} + a_1 X^{m-w-1}+...+a_{m-w}. The a_i range freely in F_p. Each fully split Lambda contributes at most one m-subset.
There are p^{m-w} coefficient choices and at most C(n,m) split m-subsets total.
B1_large_free_count.statement: Monic degree-m polynomials with a fixed length-w high monic prefix form an affine space of dimension free = m-w = 846161 over F_p (the free lower coefficients). At most p^{m-w} such polynomials exist, hence M_m^{max} <= p^{m-w}. Also M_m^{max} <= C(n,m). Both are far above the atom scale.

B2_U_res_core_prefix.name: U_res_equals_num_distinct_residual_core_prefixes_under_pencil
proof: Fix can-core C. Residual S with C_can(S)=C have U = S\\C of size e, and by the core-pencil theorem those U form a constant-shift family: identical length-w monic prefixes u, varying only the constant term. Hence one u per core.

Different cores may share a prefix b only if they collide in Fib_m(b); the map core |-> u = invert_u(z, Phi_w(C)) is therefore well-defined on residual cores, and the image size is U_res. Injectivity of core |-> u fails exactly when two residual cores share Phi_w, i.e. M_m contributions. Always U_res <= N_can_prim, with equality if residual can-cores have distinct Phi_w-prefixes.
B2_U_res_core_prefix.sparseness_law: NON-CIRCULAR REPHRASE of B2: bound the number of distinct Phi_w-prefixes among residual can-cores (an m-subset prefix-image size), not |R|. When residual can-cores form an injective Phi_w family, U_res = N_can_prim and the atom reduces to N_can_prim <= target/17.
B2_U_res_core_prefix.statement: Let R(z) be a residual (or any) subset of Fib_w(z). For each S in R(z) let C_can(S), U_lex(S) be the lex-split and u(S)=Phi_w(U_lex(S)), b(S)=Phi_w(C_can(S)). Then b(S)=invert_b(z,u(S)). Moreover all residual supports with the same can-core C share the same u (side free-1 pencil: only constant term varies). Consequently the set of residual side-prefixes and the set of residual can-core prefixes are in bijection via invert_b(z,·), and U_res(z) = |{Phi_w(C): C = C_can(S) for some S in R(z)}| <= N_can_prim(z).

B2_bijection.name: side_prefix_core_prefix_affine_bijection
proof: Product monic high coefficients give z_k = b_k + u_k + sum_{i<k} b_i u_{k-i} for k=1..w (with m,e > w at deployed).
Solving for b_k: b_k = z_k - u_k - sum_{i<k} b_i u_{k-i} (forward triangular, diag 1).
Solving for u_k: u_k = z_k - b_k - sum_{i<k} b_i u_{k-i} (same shape).
Mutual inversion is by direct substitution / induction on k.
B2_bijection.statement: Fix a j-fiber prefix z in F_p^w. The maps u |-> b = invert_b(z,u) and b |-> u = invert_u(z,b) are mutually inverse bijections F_p^w -> F_p^w. Explicitly, both are triangular with nonzero diagonal.

B2_sparseness_target.link_to_B1: If B1 holds (Phi_w injective on ALL m-subsets), then every family of m-subsets has |Phi_w-image| = |family|, so U_res = N_can_prim and B2 becomes identical to bounding N_can_prim directly.
B2_sparseness_target.name: B2_residual_core_prefix_image
B2_sparseness_target.statement: Prove that the set of Phi_w-prefixes of residual can-cores at each z has size <= 16166878605395467 (K_rem form) or <= 8456648496904 (t*p form). This does not go through |R|; it is an m-subset prefix-image bound on a residual-selected family of m-subsets.
B2_sparseness_target.why_not_circular: The family of residual can-cores has size N_can_prim, which may be << |R| when many residual supports share cores (up to pack_ceil=17 per core). Bounding the Phi_w-image of that family is a pure m-subset problem on a structured subfamily, not a bound on j-subset residual mass.

title: Large-free B1 injectivity equivalence and B2 bijective core-prefix sparseness law
